in the present month of financial
strenuosity at the city hall one of
the bills that the city fahers will
have to meet is the charge of $1112
dae the Washington Water Power
company for arc light service.


Z This bill is no different from what
IL has been for a long time past, but
IL looks unusually large just at pres
cnt. The only consolation the city
dads can get from looking at the bill
Is the realization that it is just half
what the bill would have been had not
the lighting company reduced its
charge when the city threatened to
put in it's own lighting plant Fur
thermore. the charge. 81 per light
per month is said to be as low as it
In in most cities.


But the chief reason for thought
mlness in the matter is that the city
mas authorized the establishment of
NJ more lights during the coming
month. five lights per ward, or an ad-
aftional cost per month of 500. With
the addition of these rs lights the
total cost per month to the city will
be S1212. or $1l.544 per year.


The question at the city hall is,
"Where is the money coming from
10 pay for these lights, and would it
aot bet better for the city to put in
an extra wheel and motor at the up
river pumping station and manufac-
ture it's own lights for the city hall
as well as for the streets?'
